summary: Strong jams with epic Bertha→Playin'→Ship and tight first set
blurb: The show opens solidly with a standout Friend Of The Devil and Althea before launching into an iconic Bertha → Playing In The Band → Ship Of Fools sequence that spills into an extended post‑Ship jam. Jerry’s voice is great and his guitar fires on eight cylinders, while Brent adds texture with a keyboard jam. Overall the second set delivers high‑energy improvisation despite a few rough spots.
review: April 5, 1982 at The Spectrum captured the Dead in a wonderfully loose yet focused mode. The first set rolls through classics—Jack Straw, Friend Of The Devil, Althea—and stays tight, with Jerry’s voice described as “great” and his guitar “firing on eight cylinders.”  The real highlight erupts in the second set: Bertha segues into Playing In The Band and then Ship Of Fools, launching an extended post‑Ship jam that carries through Drums and Space before exploding back into a second Playin’ and The Wheel.  Brent’s “Brent Jam #1” adds a lush keyboard texture, and both Mickey and Bill are praised for powerful, intricate drumming throughout the improvisations.  While the audience recording has minor glitches—missing seconds at the set break and occasional crowd interruptions—the overall performance is solid, with only a few less‑inspired moments (notably Good Lovin’).  Fans looking for the quintessential Spectrum energy should give this show a spin; the jams are memorable and the band’s chemistry shines.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings describe the performance as mixed but lean positive: standout jams (Bertha→Playin'→Ship), great vocal and guitar work, and strong rhythm section are highlighted repeatedly, while only minor flaws are noted. The consistency of praise across multiple sources justifies a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape with good balance and crisp detail that captures the epic Bertha→Playin'→Ship sequence despite minor gaps; generally regarded as the clearest, most complete version.

key_highlights: Epic Bertha → Playing In The Band → Ship Of Fools sequence with extended post‑Ship jam into Drums/Space
Standout Friend Of The Devil and Althea in the first set
Brent Mydland’s keyboard “jam #1” adds texture to the second‑set improvisations
Jerry’s voice described as great and his guitar firing on eight cylinders

band_performance.Jerry: His voice is great and his guitar work fires on eight cylinders, leading the jams with confidence.
band_performance.Phil: Bass sits well in the mix; noted for swapping places with Jerry during the drummers’ segment, providing solid foundation throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Adds texture with a notable keyboard jam (“Brent Jam #1”) and blends nicely into the overall sound.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides powerful drumming, especially in the post‑Ship jam that drives the extended improvisation.
band_performance.Billy: His hi‑hat work is highlighted as a major strength; contributes strongly to the rhythmic drive of the second set.
